rule_logic: Primary locked source used for methodology documentation is treatment_menu_recommendation_sheet_final_locked.csv.
methodology_note: This file captures the locked end-state used to document pricing logic assumptions.

rule_logic: Low values are rounded down to the nearest 10. High values are rounded up to the nearest 10. Mid anchors are rounded to the nearest 10 for commercial consistency.
methodology_note: Decile rounding is used to remove irregular price endings and improve menu readability.

rule_logic: Recommended reference bands are centered on the selected tier midpoint rather than using the full low-high envelope from the tier.
methodology_note: This avoids overly wide recommendation ranges such as 50-110 or 60-130.

rule_logic: Band width is determined by duration. Shorter treatments use tighter commercial bands; longer treatments use wider bands.
methodology_note: The implemented workflow ultimately produced 45-minute entry bands and 60+/75-minute wider bands.

rule_logic: If a rounded band collapses into a single value or an invalid range, force a minimum spread so low < high and no repeated-value bands remain.
methodology_note: Used to eliminate bands such as 79-79 or 89-89.

rule_logic: Tier family is inferred from treatment positioning narrative. In practice, material-intensity narratives and labor-led narratives were both translated into commercially readable recommendation bands and then narrowed into final menu prices.
methodology_note: Narrative inputs influenced band selection logic before commercial harmonization.

rule_logic: Final recommended price points are chosen from within the recommended reference band using commercially familiar 9-ending prices where possible.
methodology_note: The final state converged to 69, 79, 89, 99, and 109 depending on band and ladder logic.

rule_logic: Every final recommended price point must remain inside the assigned recommended reference band.
methodology_note: The locked final file shows all rows validated successfully.

rule_logic: Pricing position labels are assigned from final price points to simplify menu architecture.
methodology_note: This creates a clean menu segmentation structure for commercial presentation.

rule_logic: Commercial summary price points use the median anchor rather than the simple arithmetic mean when a market-facing price must be displayed.
methodology_note: This is why 60-minute core pricing was commercialized to 89 instead of 87.

rule_logic: When a longer duration within the same pricing position label does not exceed the shorter duration price, increase the longer duration by one commercial step.
methodology_note: This creates a credible price ladder where longer durations do not flatten against shorter ones.

rule_logic: Material cost ratios are assigned using treatment profile cues. Material-heavy treatments use a higher ratio than labor-led treatments.
methodology_note: Material-heavy therapies absorb a larger consumable cost share.

rule_logic: Labor cost ratios are higher for labor-and-time led treatments, with an extra uplift for longer sessions.
methodology_note: Longer therapist time directly increases labor exposure.

rule_logic: Overhead cost ratio varies by session duration, increasing modestly as treatment time increases.
methodology_note: This is a proxy assumption, not a direct accounting extraction.

rule_logic: Gross profit proxy per treatment equals price minus material cost proxy minus labor cost proxy.
methodology_note: Gross profit proxy excludes overhead in this simplified pricing model.

rule_logic: Gross margin proxy percent equals gross profit proxy divided by the selected price basis.
methodology_note: Gross margin proxy is a simplified economic indicator for menu comparison.

rule_logic: Net profit proxy per treatment equals price minus material cost proxy minus labor cost proxy minus overhead cost proxy.
methodology_note: Net profit proxy is still a pricing model estimate, not an audited net margin.

rule_logic: Net margin proxy percent equals net profit proxy divided by the selected price basis.
methodology_note: Used to compare treatment economics after final menu-price harmonization.

rule_logic: When ladder harmonization changes the displayed menu price, economics must be recalculated from the menu price rather than reusing base-price economics.
parameter_3_value: recompute menu-based cost and profit proxies
example_input: price architecture changes final displayed menu price
example_output: menu economics refreshed from new price basis
methodology_note: This keeps pricing architecture and profitability analysis on the same basis.

rule_logic: Final locked menu recommendation output is treatment_menu_recommendation_sheet_final_locked.csv.
methodology_note: This methodology CSV documents the logic behind the locked treatment pricing state.
